case_title: JESSICA JIMENEZ VS. MENZIES AVIATION, INC. et al

docket_entries.description: OTHER NON EXEMPT COMPLAINTS, COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF JIMENEZ, JESSICA INDIVIDUALLY ON BEHALF OF ALL OTHER CURRENT AND FORMER SIMILARLY SITUATED CALIFORNIA EMPLOYEES OF DEFENDANTS AS TO DEFENDANT MENZIES AVIATION, INC. MENZIES AVIATION GROUP (USA), INC. DOES 1 THRU 10, INCL. SUMMONS ISSUED, JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE SCHEDULED FOR NOV-05-2010 PROOF OF SERVICE DUE ON                  AUG-02-2010 CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT DUE ON         OCT-21-2010 COMPLEX LITIGATION ASSIGNMENT REQUESTED BY FILING PARTIES; FEE INCLUDED IN FILING FEE CLASS ACTION
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF NOV-05-2010 IS OFF CALENDAR, NOTICE OF REMOVAL. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS MENZIES AVIATION, INC. & MENZIES AVIATION GROUP (USA), INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED IN PART. THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST THROUGH TENTH CAUSES OF ACTION ON THE GROUNDS THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT STATE A VIABLE CLAIM IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD, IF SHE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, FACTS SUFFICIENT TO STATE A CLAIM UNDER MORILLION V. ROYAL PACKING CO. (2000) 22 CAL.4TH 575 AND OVERTON V. WALT DISNEY CO. (2006) 136 CAL.APP.4TH 263. AS CURRENTLY PLED, THE ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT ARE UNCLEAR. PLAINTIFF PLEADS THAT EMPLOYEES OF DEFENDANTS WERE REQUIRED TO PARK IN DESIGNATED LOTS AND TAKE A SHUTTLE TO THEIR WORKING LOCATION, BUT ELSEWHERE PLEADS FACTS WHICH TEND TO INDICATE THAT PLAINTIFFS WERE PERMITTED TO TAKE OTHER FORMS OF TRANSPORTATION TO WORK. THE DEMURRER ON ALL OTHER GROUNDS IS OVERRULED. REGARDING THE POSSIBILITY OF CLASS CERTIFICATION ON BOTH THE COMMUTING THEORY AND THE UNIFORM THEORY, PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS SHOW A REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT PLAINTIFF CAN ESTABLISH A COMMUNITY OF INTEREST AND AN ASCERTAINABLE CLASS. (SEE CANON USA, INC. V. SUP. CT. (1998) 68 CAL.APP.4TH 1, 5; ALVAREZ V. THE MAY DEPT. STORES CO. (2006) 143 CAL.APP.4TH 1223, 1231-32.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

Off Calendar Oct-28-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANTS MENZIES AVIATION, INC. and MENZIES AVIATION GROUP (USA), INCORPORATED'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - Further briefing due in 5 court days and 5 days for response from defendant. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS MENZIES AVIATION, INC. & MENZIES AVIATION GROUP (USA), INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled in part and sustained in part. The demurrer to the first through tenth causes of action on the grounds that Plaintiff cannot state a viable claim is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead, if she can do so in good faith, facts sufficient to state a claim under Morillion v. Royal Packing Co. (2000) 22 Cal.4th 575 and Overton v. Walt Disney Co. (2006) 136 Cal.App.4th 263. As currently pled, the allegations in the Complaint are unclear. Plaintiff pleads that employees of Defendants were required to park in designated lots and take a shuttle to their working location, but elsewhere pleads facts which tend to indicate that Plaintiffs were permitted to take other forms of transportation to work. The demurrer on all other grounds is overruled. Regarding the possibility of class certification on both the commuting theory and the uniform theory, Plaintiff's allegations show a reasonable probability that Plaintiff can establish a community of interest and an ascertainable class. (See Canon USA, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1998) 68 Cal.App.4th 1, 5; Alvarez v. The May Dept. Stores Co. (2006) 143 Cal.App.4th 1223, 1231-32.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/EHG)
